items.headline: Two runners break two-hour marathon barrier in record-eligible race
items.body: Sabastian Sawe ran 1:59:30 at the London Marathon on April 26, becoming the first person to break two hours in official conditions. Millions of distance runners now train toward a barrier previously considered impossible outside exhibition attempts.

items.headline: Mali defense minister killed in nationwide coordinated attacks
items.body: General Sadio Camara died when fighters drove a car bomb into his residence on April 30. Mali loses a central figure in its ruling military government amid expanding insurgent control.

items.headline: United Arab Emirates withdraws from OPEC effective May 1
items.body: The UAE announced its departure from the oil cartel on May 1, removing 4.8 million barrels per day of production capacity. Global oil supply coordination weakens as the group loses its third-largest producer.

items.headline: Direct commercial flights resume between United States and Venezuela after seven years
items.body: American Airlines flight AA3599 landed in Caracas on April 30, ending a suspension since 2019. Hundreds of thousands of Venezuelans in the United States gain direct access to family without third-country connections.

es.headline: Israel seizes Gaza-bound flotilla with 31 Spanish nationals in international waters near Greece
es.body: Israeli forces intercepted over 20 boats sailing from Barcelona on Wednesday, detaining 175 activists total. Spain suspends diplomatic engagement and demands EU freeze Israel association agreement immediately.

nl.headline: Netherlands requests international wildfire assistance through EU system for first time in history
nl.body: Fire departments exhausted their resources fighting blazes across seven locations and activated EU Civil Protection Mechanism on April 30. France and Germany deployed specialized wildfire vehicles and helicopter while Belgium sent crews to prevent peat reignition.
